OpenAgents represents a thoughtful approach to AI-assisted development that prioritizes safety without sacrificing productivity. Its plan-first, approval-based workflow addresses legitimate concerns about autonomous AI behavior while enabling developers to leverage AI’s capabilities confidently.

As AI tools become more powerful, frameworks like OpenAgents that provide guardrails and oversight will be essential for responsible AI adoption in software development. Whether you’re an individual developer or part of an enterprise team, OpenAgents offers a path to AI-assisted development that doesn’t require surrendering control.

Resources

Section titled “Resources”
  • GitHub: github.com/darrenhinde/OpenAgents
  • OpenCode: opencode.ai
  • Documentation: Agent System Blueprint
  • Roadmap: ROADMAP.md

Tags: #AI #OpenSource #DeveloperTools #AISafety #OpenAgents #OpenCode #PlanFirstDevelopment

Tags:
  • AI
  • Developer Tools
  • Open Source
  • AI Safety

UI/UX Pro Max: A Searchable Design Intelligence Skill for Building Professional UIs

Jan 1, 2026
Vibe Sparking

Introduction

Section titled “Introduction”

Building beautiful, professional user interfaces is both an art and a science. As developers, we often struggle with design decisions - which colors work best for a SaaS dashboard? What font pairings convey professionalism? How do you implement glassmorphism effects correctly?

Enter UI/UX Pro Max - a comprehensive AI skill that transforms your coding assistant into a design expert. Created by nextlevelbuilder, this open-source tool provides a searchable database of design intelligence that works seamlessly with popular AI coding assistants.

What is UI/UX Pro Max?

Section titled “What is UI/UX Pro Max?”

UI/UX Pro Max is a searchable design database that provides instant access to professional UI/UX knowledge. Think of it as having a senior designer looking over your shoulder, ready to suggest the perfect color palette, typography combination, or UX pattern for your specific use case.

What’s Inside

Section titled “What’s Inside”

The skill contains a massive collection of design resources:

CategoryCount
UI Styles57 variations
Color Palettes95 industry-specific collections
Font Pairings56 curated combinations
Chart Types24 dashboard recommendations
Tech Stacks8 framework-specific guidelines
UX Guidelines98 best practices and rules

How It Works

Section titled “How It Works”

The workflow is brilliantly simple:

  1. You ask - Request any UI/UX task (build, design, create, implement, review, fix, improve)
  2. Skill activates - The AI automatically searches the design database
  3. Smart recommendations - Based on your product type, it finds matching design systems
  4. Code generation - Implements UI with proper colors, fonts, spacing, and best practices

Supported AI Assistants

Section titled “Supported AI Assistants”

UI/UX Pro Max is designed to work across multiple platforms:

AI AssistantInstallation
Claude Code.claude/skills/ui-ux-pro-max/
Cursor.cursor/commands/ui-ux-pro-max.md + .shared/ui-ux-pro-max/
Windsurf.windsurf/workflows/ui-ux-pro-max.md + .shared/ui-ux-pro-max/
Antigravity.agent/workflows/ui-ux-pro-max.md + .shared/ui-ux-pro-max/
GitHub Copilot.github/prompts/ui-ux-pro-max.prompt.md + .shared/ui-ux-pro-max/
Kiro.kiro/steering/ui-ux-pro-max.md + .shared/ui-ux-pro-max/

Installation

Section titled “Installation”

Quick Install (CLI)

Section titled “Quick Install (CLI)”

Manual Installation

Section titled “Manual Installation”

Copy the appropriate folders to your project based on your AI assistant (see table above).

Prerequisites: Python 3.x is required for the search script.

Usage Examples

Section titled “Usage Examples”

Claude Code

Section titled “Claude Code”

The skill activates automatically when you request UI/UX work:

Or use the slash command:

Example Prompts

Section titled “Example Prompts”
  • Build a landing page for my SaaS product
  • Create a dashboard for healthcare analytics
  • Design a portfolio website with dark mode
  • Make a mobile app UI for e-commerce

Supported Tech Stacks

Section titled “Supported Tech Stacks”

UI/UX Pro Max provides stack-specific guidelines for:

  • HTML + Tailwind (default)
  • React / Next.js
  • Vue / Svelte
  • SwiftUI / React Native / Flutter

Just mention your preferred stack in your prompt, or let it default to HTML + Tailwind.

What Makes It Special

Section titled “What Makes It Special”

Industry-Specific Color Palettes

Section titled “Industry-Specific Color Palettes”

The skill includes color palettes tailored to specific industries:

  • SaaS - Professional blues, trustworthy grays
  • E-commerce - Conversion-optimized palettes
  • Healthcare - Calming greens, clean whites
  • Fintech - Secure blues, authoritative darks
  • Beauty - Elegant pastels, sophisticated accents

Curated Font Pairings

Section titled “Curated Font Pairings”

No more agonizing over font combinations. Each pairing includes:

  • Google Fonts import statements
  • Recommended usage (headings vs body)
  • Visual hierarchy guidelines

Comprehensive UI Styles

Section titled “Comprehensive UI Styles”

From modern trends to timeless classics:

  • Glassmorphism
  • Claymorphism
  • Minimalism
  • Brutalism
  • Neumorphism
  • Bento Grid
  • Dark Mode
  • And 50+ more…

UX Best Practices

Section titled “UX Best Practices”

The skill includes 98 UX guidelines covering:

  • Accessibility rules (WCAG compliance)
  • Responsive design patterns
  • Navigation best practices
  • Form design guidelines
  • Anti-patterns to avoid

Real-World Impact

Section titled “Real-World Impact”

For developers who aren’t professional designers, UI/UX Pro Max fills a crucial gap:

  1. Eliminates decision paralysis - No more staring at blank canvases
  2. Ensures consistency - Industry-standard patterns and spacing
  3. Saves time - Instant recommendations vs. hours of research
  4. Improves quality - Professional-level design decisions
  5. Teaches principles - Learn by seeing expert choices in action

Open Source and Community

Section titled “Open Source and Community”

UI/UX Pro Max is licensed under the MIT License, making it free to use, modify, and distribute. The project is actively developed and welcomes contributions.

  • GitHub: nextlevelbuilder/ui-ux-pro-max-skill
  • Website: ui-ux-pro-max-skill.nextlevelbuilder.io
  • Language: Python (77.3%), TypeScript (19.9%), JavaScript (2.8%)

Conclusion

Section titled “Conclusion”

Whether you’re a solo developer building a startup, a frontend engineer at a large company, or anyone in between, UI/UX Pro Max is a game-changer. It brings professional design intelligence to your fingertips, integrated seamlessly into your existing AI-assisted development workflow.

Instead of struggling with design decisions or spending hours researching best practices, you can now get instant, context-aware recommendations that help you build beautiful, professional user interfaces.

The combination of comprehensive design resources, multi-platform support, and intelligent context-awareness makes UI/UX Pro Max an essential addition to any developer’s toolkit.

References:

  • UI/UX Pro Max GitHub Repository
  • Official Website
  • Star History
Tags:
  • AI
  • Developer Tools
  • Open Source
  • UI/UX
  • Design

Apple App Store Review Rejection: A Painful Lesson from Carelessly Submitting the Wrong Version

Dec 31, 2025
Vibe Sparking

Apple App Store Review Rejection: A Painful Lesson from Carelessly Submitting the Wrong Version

Section titled “Apple App Store Review Rejection: A Painful Lesson from Carelessly Submitting the Wrong Version”

“Double-check your submission version. Don’t let carelessness ruin your hard work.” — A painful lesson learned.

As an iOS developer, the App Store review process is challenging enough. But the most frustrating rejections aren’t due to code bugs or design issues—they’re due to your own carelessness.

Today I want to share a lesson that left me full of regret.

Background: Resubmitting After UI Fixes

Section titled “Background: Resubmitting After UI Fixes”

In a previous article, I documented the entire process of using Google Antigravity to fix App Store review issues for MoneyMind. After much effort, I finally fixed all the flagged UI problems:

  • Hidden the non-functional edit icon
  • Fixed the text overlay issue
  • Ensured page style consistency

Full of confidence, I prepared a new version and resubmitted for review.

First Resubmission

Section titled “First Resubmission”

After receiving Apple’s review issue email, I followed the standard submission process:

  1. Open Xcode
  2. Update version number
  3. Archive the project
  4. Upload to App Store Connect
  5. Fill in review information

Everything seemed smooth, and I awaited the review results with anticipation.

The Email Returned: Same Issues

Section titled “The Email Returned: Same Issues”

A few days later, when I saw that familiar Apple email again, my heart sank.

Same review team, same rejection reasons, same UI issue screenshots.

How could this be? I clearly fixed all the issues!

The Truth: Submitted the Wrong Version

Section titled “The Truth: Submitted the Wrong Version”

When I logged into App Store Connect and carefully examined the submitted version, the truth made me want to hide:

The version I submitted didn’t contain any fixes at all!

In my haste, I didn’t carefully check:

  • Whether the submitted Build version corresponded to the fixed code
  • Didn’t do a final Smoke Test before submission
  • Didn’t carefully verify the consistency between Git commit and Xcode Archive

Damage Assessment

Section titled “Damage Assessment”

This careless error brought real losses:

Time Cost

Section titled “Time Cost”
  • Lost a valuable review opportunity (usually takes 1-3 days)
  • Need to requeue for a new review
  • Delayed the release of the bug fix version to users

Opportunity Cost

Section titled “Opportunity Cost”
  • Users continue using the version with UI issues
  • Affected app user experience and ratings
  • Missed potential version promotion timing

Mental Burden

Section titled “Mental Burden”
  • Self-blame and regret
  • Potentially leaving a bad impression on Apple’s review team
  • Lost confidence in my own workflow

How to Avoid the Same Mistake

Section titled “How to Avoid the Same Mistake”

From this lesson, I’ve compiled a pre-submission checklist to help other developers avoid the same error:

1. Version Verification Checklist

Section titled “1. Version Verification Checklist”

2. Create Verification Scripts

Section titled “2. Create Verification Scripts”

To ensure the submitted version is correct, I wrote a simple verification script:

3. Record Submission Information

Section titled “3. Record Submission Information”

Create a submission record document for each submission:

Submitting the Correct Version and Explaining

Section titled “Submitting the Correct Version and Explaining”

After discovering the error, I immediately took the following actions:

  1. Re-Archive the correct code version
  2. Upload new Build to App Store Connect
  3. Honestly explain the situation in review information

In the review information, I wrote:

“Dear Apple Review Team:

In my previous submission, I mistakenly uploaded the wrong version without the fixes. This submission includes all fixed issues:

  1. Hidden the non-functional edit icon
  2. Fixed text overlay issues
  3. Ensured page style consistency with other pages

I sincerely apologize for the extra work this caused. Thank you for your understanding.”

Being honest about the mistake is better than pretending nothing happened. Apple’s review team is human too—they understand that developers make mistakes.

Lessons Learned

Section titled “Lessons Learned”

This experience made me deeply realize:

  1. Carefulness is the most important quality — Technical skills can be learned, but carelessness requires constant vigilance
  2. Processes are more reliable than trust — Establish strict checklists, don’t rely on “it should be fine”
  3. Verification is a necessary step — Always verify your version before submitting
  4. Honesty when facing mistakes — If you make a mistake, admit it honestly and correct it

Advice for Other Developers

Section titled “Advice for Other Developers”

If you’re preparing an App Store submission, remember:

  1. Slow is fast — Spending 10 extra minutes checking is better than wasting days on re-review
  2. Trust your checklist — Even for simple tasks, confirm each item
  3. Document everything — Keep records of each submission for easy traceability
  4. Don’t be overconfident — Even experienced developers make mistakes

Conclusion

Section titled “Conclusion”

While this App Store rejection experience was painful, it was also a valuable lesson. It reminded me that in software development, technical ability is only part of success—meticulousness and a rigorous attitude are equally important.

I hope my story helps other developers avoid the same mistake. Remember: Double-check your submission version. Don’t let carelessness ruin your hard work.

Wishing everyone smooth App Store reviews!

Related Articles

Section titled “Related Articles”
  • Quickly Fix App Store Review Issues with Google Antigravity: A Real Case Study

Last updated: December 31, 2025

pg-aiguide: AI-Optimized PostgreSQL Expertise for Coding Assistants

Dec 31, 2025
Vibe Sparking

pg-aiguide: AI-Optimized PostgreSQL Expertise for Coding Assistants

Section titled “pg-aiguide: AI-Optimized PostgreSQL Expertise for Coding Assistants”

In the rapidly evolving landscape of AI-powered development tools, one challenge has persisted: AI coding assistants often generate database code that is outdated, missing critical constraints, unaware of modern PostgreSQL features, and inconsistent with real-world best practices.

Enter pg-aiguide – an innovative MCP server and Claude plugin developed by Timescale (now Tiger Data) that provides AI-optimized PostgreSQL expertise to coding assistants.

What is pg-aiguide?

Section titled “What is pg-aiguide?”

pg-aiguide is an open-source tool (Apache-2.0 licensed) that helps AI coding tools write dramatically better PostgreSQL code. It provides three core capabilities:

  1. Semantic Search across the official PostgreSQL manual (version-aware)
  2. AI-Optimized Skills – curated, opinionated Postgres best practices used automatically by AI agents
  3. Extension Ecosystem Docs, starting with TimescaleDB, with more extensions coming soon

The Problem pg-aiguide Solves

Section titled “The Problem pg-aiguide Solves”

AI coding tools frequently generate PostgreSQL code that suffers from several issues:

  • Outdated syntax – not keeping up with modern PostgreSQL versions
  • Missing constraints and indexes – leading to poor data integrity and performance
  • Unaware of modern PG features – missing out on capabilities introduced in recent versions
  • Inconsistent with best practices – not following proven patterns from experienced developers

Real-World Impact

Section titled “Real-World Impact”

The pg-aiguide team conducted an impressive comparison test. When asked to create an e-commerce database schema:

  • 4× more constraints compared to AI without pg-aiguide
  • 55% more indexes (including partial and expression indexes)
  • PG17-recommended patterns – using the latest PostgreSQL features
  • Modern features like GENERATED ALWAYS AS IDENTITY and NULLS NOT DISTINCT
  • Cleaner naming & documentation – more maintainable code

Installation & Setup

Section titled “Installation & Setup”

pg-aiguide can be used in two ways:

As a Public MCP Server

Section titled “As a Public MCP Server”

Available at https://mcp.tigerdata.com/docs, it can be configured for any AI coding agent:

As a Claude Code Plugin

Section titled “As a Claude Code Plugin”

For Claude Code users, installation is straightforward:

Supported AI Tools

Section titled “Supported AI Tools”

pg-aiguide supports a wide range of AI coding environments:

  • Claude Code – Native plugin support
  • Cursor – One-click install available
  • Gemini CLI – MCP server integration
  • VS Code / VS Code Insiders – Command-line installation
  • Windsurf – Configuration-based setup
  • Codex – MCP server integration

Key Features

Section titled “Key Features”

Semantic Documentation Search

Section titled “Semantic Documentation Search”

The semantic_search_postgres_docs function performs semantic search over the official PostgreSQL manual, with results scoped to specific Postgres versions. This means AI agents get accurate, version-relevant information.

Extension Ecosystem Support

Section titled “Extension Ecosystem Support”

Through semantic_search_tiger_docs, pg-aiguide searches Tiger Data’s documentation corpus, including TimescaleDB and future ecosystem extensions.

Curated Best Practices

Section titled “Curated Best Practices”

The view_skill function exposes opinionated PostgreSQL best-practice skills covering:

  • Schema design patterns
  • Indexing strategies
  • Data type selection
  • Data integrity and constraints
  • Naming conventions
  • Performance tuning
  • Modern PostgreSQL features

Use Cases

Section titled “Use Cases”

Simple Schema Design

Section titled “Simple Schema Design”

Prompt: “Create a Postgres table schema for storing usernames and unique email addresses.”

pg-aiguide helps generate properly constrained schemas with appropriate indexes and modern PostgreSQL types.

Complex IoT Schema

Section titled “Complex IoT Schema”

For more complex scenarios, like an IoT device monitoring system:

“Generate a Postgres schema for an IoT device company. Devices collect environmental data including temperature, humidity, pressure, and other measurements. Each device has a unique ID and human-readable name. Analysis includes finding outliers, anomalies, and device-specific ad-hoc analysis.”

pg-aiguide helps design schemas optimized for both recent data analysis and historical reporting.

Technology Stack

Section titled “Technology Stack”
  • Python (66.5%) – Core server logic
  • TypeScript (23.1%) – Plugin integrations
  • JavaScript (9.3%) – Web interfaces

Future Plans

Section titled “Future Plans”

The project has an ambitious roadmap:

  • pgvector support for vector similarity search
  • PostGIS for geospatial data
  • Community contributions for additional extensions

Contributing

Section titled “Contributing”

pg-aiguide welcomes contributions in several areas:

  • New Postgres best-practice skills
  • Additional documentation corpora
  • Search quality improvements
  • Bug reports and feature ideas

Conclusion

Section titled “Conclusion”

pg-aiguide represents a significant step forward in AI-assisted database development. By providing AI agents with deep, versioned PostgreSQL knowledge and proven patterns, it bridges the gap between AI-generated code and production-quality database schemas.

Whether you’re a developer using AI coding assistants or a team building AI-powered development tools, pg-aiguide offers a practical solution to the persistent problem of suboptimal AI-generated database code.

Links

Section titled “Links”
  • GitHub: timescale/pg-aiguide
  • MCP Server: https://mcp.tigerdata.com/docs
  • License: Apache-2.0
  • Author: Timescale (Tiger Data)

Published: December 31, 2025

Tags:
  • postgresql
  • ai
  • mcp
  • claude-code
  • database
  • developer-tools
  • timescale

Pluely: The Open Source AI Assistant That's 27x Lighter Than Cluely

Dec 31, 2025
Vibe Sparking

In the world of AI meeting assistants, one name has been making waves: Cluely. Marketed as the “#1 Undetectable AI for Meetings,” Cluely raised $15M in funding to build a stealth AI assistant that helps you during meetings, interviews, and conversations—completely invisibly.

But what if you want the same powerful features without the proprietary lock-in, the hefty price tag, or the 270MB application size?

Enter Pluely—the open-source alternative that’s just 10MB, privacy-first, and built for the community.

Why Pluely Matters

Section titled “Why Pluely Matters”

Pluely represents a growing movement in the AI space: open-source, privacy-first alternatives to commercial AI tools. As developers and users become increasingly concerned about data privacy, vendor lock-in, and bloated applications, projects like Pluely offer a compelling alternative.

The Numbers Don’t Lie

Section titled “The Numbers Don’t Lie”
FeaturePluely (Open Source)Cluely (Proprietary)
App Size~10MB~270MB
Size Difference27x smallerBaseline
Compute Usage50% less CPU/RAMHeavy resource usage
Startup Time<100msSeveral seconds
Privacy100% local with your LLMData sent to servers
CostFree & Open SourceCommercial pricing
LicenseGPL-3.0Proprietary

Source: Pluely GitHub

Built with Tauri: Why It Matters

Section titled “Built with Tauri: Why It Matters”

Pluely is built with Tauri, a modern framework for building desktop applications using web technologies. This architectural choice is significant:

Tauri vs Electron Performance

Section titled “Tauri vs Electron Performance”

Based on 2024-2025 benchmarks:

  • Memory Usage: Tauri uses 50-58% less memory than Electron equivalents
  • Startup Time: Tauri apps launch in under 500ms vs ~4 seconds for Electron
  • Bundle Size: Tauri produces substantially smaller bundles by using native OS webview components instead of bundling Chromium

Sources:

  • Tauri vs Electron Comparison
  • Tauri vs Electron 2025 Comparison

Why This Matters for Users

Section titled “Why This Matters for Users”

For an AI assistant that needs to run constantly in the background:

  1. Less battery drain on laptops
  2. More resources available for other applications
  3. Instant startup when you need quick answers
  4. Smaller downloads and updates

Core Features

Section titled “Core Features”

1. Invisibility Mode

Section titled “1. Invisibility Mode”

Pluely’s signature feature is its translucent overlay window that sits above all other applications. This makes it:

  • Invisible in video calls (Zoom, Google Meet, Teams, Slack)
  • Undetectable in screen shares and recordings
  • Screenshot-proof by design
  • Perfectly stealthy during interviews and presentations

2. Real-Time Audio Capture

Section titled “2. Real-Time Audio Capture”

The system audio capture feature allows you to:

  • Record audio directly from your computer’s output
  • Transcribe meetings in real-time
  • Use voice activity detection for efficient processing

3. Multi-Provider AI Support

Section titled “3. Multi-Provider AI Support”

Unlike many alternatives that lock you into one provider, Pluely supports:

Pre-configured Providers:

  • OpenAI (GPT models)
  • Anthropic (Claude)
  • Google (Gemini)
  • xAI (Grok)
  • Mistral AI
  • Cohere
  • Perplexity
  • Groq
  • Ollama (local models)

Custom Providers: Add any LLM provider using curl commands with full flexibility for authentication, streaming, and response parsing.

4. Voice Input & Screenshots

Section titled “4. Voice Input & Screenshots”
  • Voice Input: Record and convert speech to text using multiple STT providers (OpenAI Whisper, ElevenLabs, Groq, etc.)
  • Screenshot Capture: Full screen or selection mode with manual or automatic AI processing
  • File Attachments: Attach documents, images, and code files for AI analysis

Privacy-First Architecture

Section titled “Privacy-First Architecture”

In an era where data privacy is paramount, Pluely takes a fundamentally different approach:

Local Data Storage

Section titled “Local Data Storage”
  • Conversations: Stored locally in SQLite on your device
  • Settings: Stored in browser localStorage, never transmitted
  • Credentials: Encrypted secure storage for API keys
  • No Telemetry: Zero analytics, usage tracking, or data collection

Direct API Connections

Section titled “Direct API Connections”

Pluely makes API calls directly from your device to your chosen AI provider:

  • No proxy servers
  • No middleware
  • No data collection
  • Verifiable via browser developer tools

Use Cases

Section titled “Use Cases”

Pluely shines in scenarios where you need AI assistance without detection:

  1. Job Interviews: Get real-time information and suggestions
  2. Sales Calls: Access product details instantly while maintaining professionalism
  3. Technical Meetings: Reference documentation without breaking flow
  4. Educational Presentations: Learning assistance that’s invisible to your audience
  5. Design Reviews: Analyze screenshots and get suggestions
  6. Live Coding: Debug code with syntax help in stealth mode

Installation and Usage

Section titled “Installation and Usage”

Prerequisites

Section titled “Prerequisites”
  • Node.js (v18 or higher)
  • Rust (latest stable)
  • npm or yarn
  • Tauri prerequisites for your platform

Quick Start

Section titled “Quick Start”

This creates platform-specific installers:

  • macOS: .dmg
  • Windows: .msi, .exe
  • Linux: .deb, .rpm, .AppImage

The Competitive Landscape

Section titled “The Competitive Landscape”

Pluely isn’t the only player in the invisible AI assistant space. Other alternatives include:

  • LockedInAI: Desktop app for stealth interview assistance
  • Final Round AI: Coaching for job interviews with video call integration
  • Beyz AI: Stealth mode for technical and behavioral questions

However, Pluely distinguishes itself through:

  • Open source transparency (GPL-3.0 license)
  • Extremely small footprint (10MB vs competitors’ 100MB+)
  • Multi-provider flexibility (not locked into one AI service)
  • Active development with regular releases

Sources:

  • Cluely Alternatives 2025
  • Cluely Alternatives - Fellow

Ethical Considerations

Section titled “Ethical Considerations”

It’s worth addressing the elephant in the room: the ethics of invisible AI assistance.

Several sources note these tools can be used for “cheating in live conversations,” which raises legitimate concerns about:

  • Academic integrity during online exams
  • Professional authenticity in job interviews
  • Fairness in competitive scenarios

Pluely and similar tools are powerful, and with power comes responsibility. The open-source nature of Pluely at least provides transparency—you can inspect exactly what the software does, unlike proprietary black-box alternatives.

Conclusion

Section titled “Conclusion”

Pluely represents an important trend in AI development: the move toward transparent, privacy-first, open-source alternatives to commercial offerings. Its 27x smaller footprint than Cluely demonstrates that you don’t need bloated software to deliver powerful features.

Whether you choose to use Pluely or not, its existence pushes the industry toward:

  • Better privacy practices
  • More efficient software architecture
  • Greater user control
  • Open standards over lock-in

The AI assistant wars are just getting started, and open-source projects like Pluely are ensuring users have a choice.

Links

Section titled “Links”
  • Pluely Website: pluely.com
  • GitHub Repository: iamsrikanthnani/pluely
  • Cluely (Original): cluely.com
  • Tauri Framework: tauri.app

Made with ❤️ by Srikanth Nani

Tags:
  • AI
  • Tauri
  • Open Source
  • Privacy
  • Meeting Assistant

Practical Review: Building HeartPetBond App with Claude Code + BMAD + GLM-4.7

Dec 31, 2025
Vibe Sparking

Core Insight: Can Zhipu GLM-4.7 replace the expensive Opus 4.5 in the Claude Code environment? How does its 200K context window perform within the document-driven BMAD workflow? This post reveals the answers through the real-world development of the HeartPetBond app.

1. Motivation: Why This Review?

Section titled “1. Motivation: Why This Review?”

In the AI-assisted programming realm, Claude Opus 4.5 has been the undisputed king, but its high cost (approx. $15/1M input, $75/1M output) deters high-frequency usage. DeepSeek V3, while affordable, occasionally hits bottlenecks with ultra-long contexts (>128K tokens).

I aimed to verify Zhipu GLM-4.7, a strong challenger to DeepSeek V3, by developing the HeartPetBond app to test:

  • Long Context Capability: Can the nominal 200K Tokens Context support an entire project’s documentation and codebase?
  • Output Length: Can the 128K Tokens Output generate complex Flutter pages in one go?
  • Stability & Hallucination: Does logic crumble during continuous development over several days?
  • Cost-Effectiveness: How much money can it save independent developers compared to Opus 4.5?

Context: When using DeepSeek V3 with Claude Code + BMAD, I encountered errors like API Error: 400 ... maximum context length is 131072 tokens... when the context exceeded 128K. GLM-4.7’s 200K context might be the solution.

With over 10 years of Flutter experience and previous success generating the MoneyMind App using Claude Code + BMAD, I set out to see if this new model could handle the challenge.

2. Setup: Integrating GLM-4.7 into Claude Code

Section titled “2. Setup: Integrating GLM-4.7 into Claude Code”

Switching Claude Code’s “brain” is straightforward:

1. Install BMAD

Section titled “1. Install BMAD”

First, we introduce the BMAD (Building Multi-Agent Development) system, the core methodology that makes AI “understand engineering standards.”

2. Configure ZHIPU GLM-4.7

Section titled “2. Configure ZHIPU GLM-4.7”

Claude Code supports connecting external models via MCP or API adapters.

  1. Register and subscribe to the Zhipu BIGMODEL platform to get an API Key.
  2. Configure the default model in Claude Code (or related MCP settings) to point to GLM-4.7.

3. From Idea to Product: The Automated Workflow

Section titled “3. From Idea to Product: The Automated Workflow”

Following the BMAD workflow, HeartPetBond’s birth followed standard Document Driven Development. Here are the key milestones:

1. Initialize Workflow

Section titled “1. Initialize Workflow”

BMAD scanned the project state and provided a precise analysis:

2. Create Product Brief

Section titled “2. Create Product Brief”

After confirming brainstorming results, the AI generated the product definition:

It even locked in the tech stack: Flutter for cross-platform UI, GetX for lightweight state management, and Drift for local persistence.

4. PoC Verification & Performance

Section titled “4. PoC Verification & Performance”

After switching to GLM-4.7, I was most surprised by its “throughput speed” and “depth of understanding.”

1. Development Speed

Section titled “1. Development Speed”

In just 2-3 days, GLM-4.7 helped me complete 5 core pages for HeartPetBond (PoC phase):

  • Welcome Page: User onboarding.
  • Ready State: State checks.
  • Home Page: Core recording flow.
  • Settings: Complex configuration management.

The code pass rate was extremely high, rarely missing brackets or breaking logic even in deep Flutter Widget trees.

2. Token Usage

Section titled “2. Token Usage”

The cost breakdown is impressive:

  • PoC Phase Total: Approx. 300 Million (300M) tokens.
  • Estimated MVP: For 15 pages and App Store submission, estimated 3 Billion (3B) tokens.

Despite this massive volume, GLM-4.7’s cost is only about 1/7th of Opus 4.5. A Pro subscription (~¥200/month) essentially covers the MVP development needs.

5. Wrap Up

Section titled “5. Wrap Up”

The verdict from this field test is clear: Building mobile apps with GLM-4.7 + Claude Code + BMAD is entirely feasible and highly cost-effective.

✅ Pros

Section titled “✅ Pros”
  1. High Cost-Performance: GLM-4.7 is a perfect “affordable alternative” to Opus 4.5. It significantly outperforms DeepSeek V3 in daily coding tasks while remaining wallet-friendly.
  2. Long Window Stability: The 200K Context Window is real. It maintained clarity without “catastrophic forgetting” even after loading mass amounts of BMAD documentation.
  3. Engineering Ready: Works seamlessly with Claude Code’s toolchain to handle complex refactoring and debugging.

⚠️ Cons

Section titled “⚠️ Cons”
  1. Reasoning Minor Flaws: In very complex business flow designs, GLM-4.7’s reasoning chain occasionally misses a step compared to the meticulous Opus 4.5. This is manageable via human Code Review and clearer prompts.
  2. Multimodal Gaps: Current API support for multimodal inputs (images/files) is limited, requiring an additional zai-mcp-server configuration.

🔮 Final Verdict

Section titled “🔮 Final Verdict”

If you are an independent developer looking for a smart AI pair programmer that won’t break the bank, Claude Code + GLM-4.7 + BMAD is the combination to beat right now.

Life is compounding. Start building your bond today.

Tags:
  • Claude Code
  • GLM-4.7
  • BMAD
  • Flutter
  • AI Development

Superagent: AI Guardrails for Secure and Compliant Agents

Dec 31, 2025
Vibe Sparking

Superagent: AI Guardrails for Secure and Compliant Agents

Section titled “Superagent: AI Guardrails for Secure and Compliant Agents”

As AI agents and chatbots become integral to business operations, ensuring their security and compliance has never been more critical. Enter Superagent – an open-source guardrail system that provides purpose-trained models to secure AI applications in real-time.

The Challenge of AI Safety

Section titled “The Challenge of AI Safety”

Building production AI applications introduces unique security challenges:

  • Prompt injection attacks that can hijack agent behavior
  • Hallucinations that spread misinformation
  • Data leaks through inadvertent PII/PHI exposure
  • Compliance violations across regulatory frameworks
  • Tool abuse where agents execute unauthorized actions

Traditional approaches using general-purpose LLMs for safety checks introduce latency and lack the precision needed for production environments.

What is Superagent?

Section titled “What is Superagent?”

Superagent provides three purpose-trained models that work together to secure AI applications:

Guard

Section titled “Guard”

Threat Detection with 98% Accuracy

Guard detects and blocks unsafe inputs before they reach your models:

  • Prompt injections and jailbreak attempts
  • Malicious tool calls
  • Backdoors and adversarial inputs
  • Unsafe content generation

According to Superagent’s benchmarks, Guard outperforms GPT-4, Gemini 2.5 Pro, and other frontier models in threat detection – without the latency penalty of running a full LLM for safety checks.

Verify

Section titled “Verify”

Output Validation and Grounding

Verify grounds and validates model outputs against enterprise sources:

  • Fact-checking against internal documents
  • Policy compliance verification
  • Consistency validation
  • Hallucination prevention

Every generation is validated before reaching users, ensuring factual accuracy and policy alignment.

Redact

Section titled “Redact”

Real-Time Data Protection

Redact removes sensitive data from text and documents:

  • PII (Personally Identifiable Information)
  • PHI (Protected Health Information)
  • API keys and secrets
  • Custom sensitive patterns

Privacy and compliance are maintained across AI inputs and outputs without manual review.

Architecture and Performance

Section titled “Architecture and Performance”

Low-Latency Design

Section titled “Low-Latency Design”

Unlike general-purpose LLMs used for safety, Superagent’s models are purpose-trained for specific security tasks, enabling:

  • Sub-millisecond response times for Guard checks
  • Minimal overhead on AI workflows
  • High-throughput processing for enterprise scale

Open Source Foundation

Section titled “Open Source Foundation”

Superagent is MIT-licensed with 10K+ GitHub stars, providing:

  • Full transparency in safety logic
  • Community-driven improvements
  • Self-hosting capabilities
  • No vendor lock-in

Integration Options

Section titled “Integration Options”

REST API

Section titled “REST API”

The simplest integration – send any payload and receive guarded results:

Python SDK

Section titled “Python SDK”

TypeScript SDK

Section titled “TypeScript SDK”

CLI Tool

Section titled “CLI Tool”

For testing and batch processing:

MCP Server

Section titled “MCP Server”

Model Context Protocol (MCP) integration for Claude Code and other MCP-compatible agents:

Compliance Coverage

Section titled “Compliance Coverage”

Superagent maps to major compliance frameworks:

FrameworkCoverage
EU AI ActRisk classification, requirements
ISO/IEC 42001AI management system standards
NIST AI RMFRisk management framework
GDPRData protection and privacy
SOC 2Security controls
HIPAAHealthcare data protection

This mapping helps organizations build audit trails and ensure regulatory alignment across all AI models.

Use Cases

Section titled “Use Cases”

Customer-Facing Applications

Section titled “Customer-Facing Applications”

Protect chatbots and assistants from prompt injection:

Enterprise Workflows

Section titled “Enterprise Workflows”

Validate outputs against internal knowledge:

Data Pipeline Security

Section titled “Data Pipeline Security”

Automatically redact sensitive data:

Agent Safety

Section titled “Agent Safety”

Monitor autonomous agent actions:

Deployment Options

Section titled “Deployment Options”

Hosted Cloud

Section titled “Hosted Cloud”

Sign up at app.superagent.sh for:

  • Zero infrastructure management
  • Automatic updates
  • Scale handling
  • SLA guarantees

Self-Hosted

Section titled “Self-Hosted”

Deploy using Docker for:

  • Data sovereignty
  • Custom model fine-tuning
  • On-premises requirements
  • Air-gapped environments

Getting Started

Section titled “Getting Started”

  1. Get an API Key at app.superagent.sh

  2. Install the SDK:

  3. Make your first guard call:

  4. Integrate into your workflow using the method that matches your stack

Comparison with Alternatives

Section titled “Comparison with Alternatives”
ApproachLatencyAccuracyCustomization
SuperagentLow98%High
GPT-4 SafetyHigh~95%Low
Llama GuardMedium~90%Medium
Regex RulesVery Low~60%Low
Manual ReviewN/A100%High

Superagent’s purpose-trained approach offers the best balance for production use.

Best Practices

Section titled “Best Practices”
  1. Layer Your Defenses – Use Guard for inputs, Verify for outputs, and Redact for logging
  2. Context Matters – Provide relevant context for better accuracy
  3. Custom Rules – Define organization-specific threat patterns
  4. Monitor False Positives – Continuously tune based on production data
  5. Version Your Policies – Track compliance requirements over time

Resources

Section titled “Resources”
  • Website: superagent.sh
  • Documentation: docs.superagent.sh
  • GitHub: github.com/superagent-ai/superagent
  • HuggingFace: huggingface.co/superagent-ai
  • Discord: discord.gg/spZ7MnqFT4

Conclusion

Section titled “Conclusion”

Superagent fills a critical gap in the AI stack – production-grade safety infrastructure that doesn’t compromise on performance. Whether you’re building customer-facing chatbots, internal AI workflows, or autonomous agents, Superagent provides the guardrails needed to ship with confidence.

The combination of high accuracy (98%), low latency, open-source transparency, and comprehensive compliance coverage makes Superagent a compelling choice for teams serious about AI safety.

Guard. Verify. Redact. – Your AI applications deserve nothing less.

Tags:
  • ai
  • security
  • compliance
  • guardrails
  • llm
  • agent-safety
  • enterprise-ai

DeepTutor: AI-Powered Personalized Learning Assistant Based on Multi-Agent Architecture

Dec 30, 2025
Vibe Sparking
Featured

DeepTutor: AI-Powered Personalized Learning Assistant Based on Multi-Agent Architecture

Section titled “DeepTutor: AI-Powered Personalized Learning Assistant Based on Multi-Agent Architecture”

In today’s rapidly evolving landscape of AI-driven education, traditional one-size-fits-all tutoring approaches are giving way to intelligent, adaptive learning systems. Allow me to introduce DeepTutor — an open-source AI personalized learning assistant from the Data Intelligence Lab at The University of Hong Kong (HKUDS), which combines multi-agent architecture with advanced RAG capabilities to deliver a comprehensive learning experience.

What is DeepTutor?

Section titled “What is DeepTutor?”

DeepTutor is a full-stack AI learning platform that revolutionizes how students and researchers interact with educational content. Open-sourced under the AGPL-3.0 license, it offers seven interconnected modules designed to support the entire learning lifecycle, from knowledge acquisition to research ideation.

Core Advantage: DeepTutor employs a dual-loop reasoning architecture that separates the investigation process from problem solving, enabling deeper analysis and more accurate solutions compared to single-turn AI tutors.

Core Features Overview

Section titled “Core Features Overview”
FeatureDescription
Smart Knowledge BaseUpload textbooks, papers, manuals to build your own AI knowledge repository
Multi-Agent Problem SolvingDual-loop architecture integrating RAG, web search, paper search, code execution
Knowledge SimplificationTransform complex concepts into visual diagrams and step-by-step explanations
Personalized Q&AContext-aware conversations that adapt to learning progress
Intelligent Question GenerationGenerate customized quizzes and assessments
Real Exam SimulationClone exam styles for authentic practice experiences
In-Depth Research & Literature ReviewSystematic analysis and cross-domain knowledge synthesis
Novel Insight DiscoveryIdentify knowledge gaps and research directions

System Architecture

Section titled “System Architecture”

DeepTutor adopts a modern full-stack design based on agent architecture:

Module Details

Section titled “Module Details”

1. Smart Solver — Dual-Loop Problem Solving

Section titled “1. Smart Solver — Dual-Loop Problem Solving”

The Smart Solver employs a sophisticated dual-loop architecture that separates analysis from solving:

Analysis Loop

  • InvestigateAgent: Collects context and relevant materials
  • NoteAgent: Compresses findings into structured notes

Solve Loop

  • PlanAgent: Creates step-by-step solution plans
  • ManagerAgent: Coordinates overall execution
  • SolveAgent: Generates solutions using tools
  • CheckAgent: Verifies answers
  • FormatAgent: Formats final output

Core Features:

FeatureDescription
Real-time StreamingWebSocket displays reasoning process in real time
Multi-Tool IntegrationRAG (naive/hybrid), web search, code execution
Citation ManagementStructured citations with reference tracking
Persistent MemoryJSON file saves context

2. Question Generator — Custom & Mimic Modes

Section titled “2. Question Generator — Custom & Mimic Modes”

DeepTutor supports two powerful question generation approaches:

Custom Mode

  • Generate questions from knowledge base
  • Specify difficulty, type, and quantity
  • Auto-verification with no refusal logic

Mimic Mode

  • Upload reference exam PDFs
  • MinerU parses and extracts structure
  • Generate questions matching original style

3. Guided Learning — Interactive Knowledge Visualization

Section titled “3. Guided Learning — Interactive Knowledge Visualization”

Transform static learning materials into interactive experiences:

AgentResponsibility
LocateAgentIdentify 3-5 progressive knowledge points
InteractiveAgentConvert concepts into visual HTML pages
ChatAgentProvide contextual Q&A for each topic
SummaryAgentGenerate learning summary upon completion

Workflow:

  1. Select a notebook containing learning records
  2. System generates progressive learning paths
  3. Interactive visualization for each concept
  4. Contextual Q&A
  5. Receive session summary

4. Deep Research — DR-in-KG System

Section titled “4. Deep Research — DR-in-KG System”

The DR-in-KG (Deep Research in Knowledge Graph) module achieves systematic research through dynamic topic queues:

Preset Modes:

  • quick: 1-2 subtopics, 1-2 iterations
  • medium: 5 subtopics, 4 iterations
  • deep: 8 subtopics, 7 iterations
  • auto: Agent autonomously determines depth

5. Collaborative Writing — Interactive AI Writing Assistant

Section titled “5. Collaborative Writing — Interactive AI Writing Assistant”

A feature-rich Markdown editor equipped with AI enhancements:

FeatureDescription
EditAgentRewrite, shorten, expand (optional context)
Auto-AnnotationAutomatically identify key content
NarratorAgentTTS generation with multiple voice options
Multi-Format ExportMarkdown, PDF export

6. Automatic Idea Generation — Research Ideation

Section titled “6. Automatic Idea Generation — Research Ideation”

Extract knowledge points from learning materials and generate research insights:

7. Knowledge Base & Notebooks

Section titled “7. Knowledge Base & Notebooks”

Knowledge Base Management:

  • Upload PDF, Markdown, TXT documents
  • Hybrid RAG + Knowledge Graph indexing
  • Incremental document addition
  • Supports text-embedding-3-large (3072 dimensions)

Personal Notebooks:

  • Multi-notebook management
  • Unified record storage across modules
  • Categorize by type and knowledge base
  • Custom colors and icons

Installation & Configuration

Section titled “Installation & Configuration”

Requirements

Section titled “Requirements”
  • Python 3.10+
  • Node.js 18+
  • LLM API keys (OpenAI/compatible)

Quick Start

Section titled “Quick Start”

Access URLs:

ServiceURL
Frontendhttp://localhost:3782
API Docshttp://localhost:8001/docs
Health Checkhttp://localhost:8001/api/v1/knowledge/health

Demo Knowledge Base

Section titled “Demo Knowledge Base”

DeepTutor offers pre-built demo packages:

  1. Research Paper Collection — 5 papers (20-50 pages each) covering RAG and agents
  2. Data Science Textbook — 8 chapters, 296 pages of deep representation learning

Download: Google Drive

Configuration Guide

Section titled “Configuration Guide”

Main configuration files:

Agent Settings (config/agents.yaml):

Server Settings (config/main.yaml):

Use Cases

Section titled “Use Cases”
ScenarioDeepTutor Solution
Student Exam PrepUpload textbooks → Generate practice questions (mimic mode)
Researcher Literature ReviewDeep research with paper search and citation tracking
Self-Directed LearningGuided learning with interactive visualizations
Problem SolvingSmart solver with step-by-step reasoning display
Content CreationCollaborative writing with AI-assisted editing and TTS
Research IdeationAutomatically generate ideas from notebook materials

Comparison with Alternatives

Section titled “Comparison with Alternatives”
FeatureDeepTutorChatGPT/ClaudeKhan Academy
Knowledge BaseUpload your own documentsLimitedCurated content
Dual-Loop Reasoning✓✗✗
Exam Mimicry✓ (mimic mode)✗✗
Citation Tracking✓ Native supportPartial✗
Interactive Visualization✓ HTML pages✗✗
Self-Hosted✓ (AGPL-3.0)✗✗
Deep Research✓ Multi-stage✗✗
Code Execution✓ Native support✗✗

Tech Stack

Section titled “Tech Stack”
ComponentTechnology
BackendFastAPI, Python 3.10+
FrontendNext.js, React, TypeScript
Vector Storetext-embedding-3-large (3072 dimensions)
Knowledge GraphEntity-Relationship Mapping
Real-time CommunicationWebSocket Streaming
Document ParsingMinerU PDF Extraction

Data Directory Structure

Section titled “Data Directory Structure”

FAQ

Section titled “FAQ”

Q: What if port 8001 is already in use? A: Edit config/main.yaml and change server.backend_port to another port.

Q: Can I add documents to an existing knowledge base? A: Yes! Use incremental addition: python -m src.knowledge.add_documents <kb_name> --docs <new_file.pdf>

Q: How do I properly stop the service? A: Press Ctrl+C. If the port remains occupied, use lsof -i :8001 to find and terminate the process.

Q: Does it support offline operation? A: After knowledge base construction, core RAG functions can run offline. Web search and paper search require internet connection.

Open Source & Community

Section titled “Open Source & Community”
  • GitHub: github.com/HKUDS/DeepTutor
  • License: AGPL-3.0
  • Organization: Data Intelligence Lab @ HKU

Related Projects

Section titled “Related Projects”
ProjectDescription
LightRAGSimple and fast RAG
RAG-AnythingMultimodal RAG
AI-ResearcherResearch automation
AutoAgentAutonomous agents

Summary

Section titled “Summary”

DeepTutor represents a comprehensive approach to AI-driven education that goes beyond simple Q&A. By combining:

  1. Multi-Agent Architecture for specialized task handling
  2. Dual-Loop Reasoning for deep problem analysis
  3. Knowledge Graph + RAG for intelligent retrieval
  4. Seven Integrated Modules covering the complete learning lifecycle

DeepTutor provides a powerful, scalable personalized learning platform for students, educators, and researchers.

Related Resources

Section titled “Related Resources”
  • GitHub Official Repository
  • Project Documentation
  • Demo Knowledge Base
  • Configuration Guide

Cover image: DeepTutor — AI-Powered Personalized Learning Assistant Based on Multi-Agent Architecture.

Tags:
  • ai
  • education
  • learning-assistant
  • multi-agent
  • rag
  • knowledge-base
  • question-generation
  • research
  • agpl-3.0
  • hkuds

Agentic Signal: Visual AI Workflow Automation Platform with Local LLM Integration

Dec 29, 2025
Vibe Sparking

Explore Agentic Signal by Code Forge Temple — a visual drag-and-drop AI workflow automation platform that runs entirely locally with Ollama integration. Build intelligent workflows without coding, with no cloud dependencies required.

Introduction

Section titled “Introduction”

Agentic Signal is an open-source visual AI workflow automation platform created by Code Forge Temple. It enables users to build intelligent, AI-powered workflows using a drag-and-drop interface while running everything locally on their machine.

What sets Agentic Signal apart is its commitment to privacy-first local execution. By integrating with Ollama, it allows you to harness the power of large language models without sending your data to the cloud. This makes it ideal for processing sensitive documents, building personal automation workflows, or simply maintaining full control over your AI interactions.

Repository: code-forge-temple/agentic-signal Website: agentic-signal.com

Key Features

Section titled “Key Features”
FeatureDescription
Visual Drag-and-Drop InterfaceBuild workflows visually using React Flow — no coding required
Node-Based ArchitectureConnect data sources, AI processors, and outputs seamlessly
Real-time ExecutionSee your workflows run in real-time with live data flow visualization
Ollama IntegrationRun AI models locally with privacy and control
Tool CallingAI agents can execute functions and access external APIs
Structured OutputJSON schema validation for reliable AI responses
Conversation MemoryMaintain context across workflow executions
Browser AutomationWeb scraping and automation via Google Chrome integration

Quick Start

Section titled “Quick Start”

Installation

Section titled “Installation”

Agentic Signal provides pre-built releases for Windows, macOS, and Linux:

  1. Download the release package for your operating system from GitHub Releases

  2. Install Google Chrome — required for web scraping and browser automation nodes

  3. Install and Run Agentic Signal

    • Run the installer and follow the prompts
    • Windows users: When launching for the first time, your antivirus may scan the app (the installer is unsigned)

  4. Configure Ollama

    • Make sure Ollama is running with CORS enabled for the Windows app
    • Configure the Ollama Host in app settings

Prerequisites

Section titled “Prerequisites”

Required

Section titled “Required”

  • Google Chrome — Required for browser automation and web scraping nodes

    • Download from google.com/chrome

  • Ollama — Local LLM runtime

Optional (for Google Services Integration)

Section titled “Optional (for Google Services Integration)”

If you want to use Gmail, Google Drive, or Google Calendar nodes, you’ll need to set up OAuth2 credentials:

  1. Go to Google Cloud Console
  2. Create a new project or select existing one
  3. Enable the APIs you want to use (Gmail, Drive, Calendar)
  4. Create OAuth 2.0 credentials (Desktop app)
  5. Download the credentials JSON file

Node Types and Architecture

Section titled “Node Types and Architecture”

Agentic Signal uses a node-based workflow architecture where each node represents a processing unit:

Node Structure

Section titled “Node Structure”
  • Input/Output Data Connectors — Connect data flow between nodes
  • Tool Connector — Additional connectors for AI tools and functions
  • Timer Connector — Schedule automatic execution at intervals
  • Settings — Configure node-specific options
  • Error Logs — View errors when processing fails
  • Output — View processed results
  • Run — Manually trigger workflow execution

Available Node Types

Section titled “Available Node Types”
Node TypeDescription
Data SourceInput data from files, APIs, or manual entry
AI Data ProcessingProcess data with local LLMs via Ollama
AI ToolExecute function calling with AI agents
HTTP DataMake REST API calls to external services
JSON ReformatterTransform and restructure JSON data
ChartVisualize data with various chart types
Data ValidationValidate data against schemas
Data Flow SpyDebug and inspect data flow
Timer NodeSchedule periodic executions

Integrations

Section titled “Integrations”

Agentic Signal supports a growing list of integrations:

Built-in Integrations

Section titled “Built-in Integrations”
IntegrationDescription
Ollama LLMsUse local language models for text analysis and generation
Google ServicesGmail, Google Drive, Google Calendar (with OAuth2 setup)
Weather APIsReal-time weather data
Search EnginesDuckDuckGo, Brave Search
Date/Time ToolsGet current date and time
HTTP/REST APIsIntegrate any REST API via HTTP Data node

Browser Automation

Section titled “Browser Automation”
  • Web scraping capabilities
  • Form filling automation
  • Data extraction from websites
  • Screenshot capture

Daily Workflow Examples

Section titled “Daily Workflow Examples”

Example 1: AI-Powered Email Assistant

Section titled “Example 1: AI-Powered Email Assistant”

Create a workflow that:

  1. Fetches new emails from Gmail (Data Source → Gmail)
  2. Uses local LLM to categorize and prioritize (AI Data Processing)
  3. Generates summary responses (AI Tool)
  4. Saves to Google Drive for reference (HTTP Data)

Example 2: Research Automation

Section titled “Example 2: Research Automation”

Build a workflow that:

  1. Takes a research topic as input (Data Source)
  2. Searches multiple sources (DuckDuckGo, Brave Search nodes)
  3. Extracts and summarizes findings with local LLM (AI Data Processing)
  4. Formats results into a structured report (JSON Reformatter)
  5. Saves to file or sends to email (Output node)

Example 3: Weather-Based Notifications

Section titled “Example 3: Weather-Based Notifications”

Create a daily automation:

  1. Trigger at 7 AM daily (Timer Node)
  2. Fetch weather data for your location (Weather API)
  3. Generate personalized recommendation with local LLM (AI Data Processing)
  4. Display formatted output (Chart or Display node)

Configuration

Section titled “Configuration”

Ollama Configuration

Section titled “Ollama Configuration”

For the desktop app to communicate with Ollama:

Then in Agentic Signal app settings:

  • Set Ollama Host to http://localhost:11434
  • Select your preferred model from the dropdown

Chrome Path Configuration

Section titled “Chrome Path Configuration”

For browser automation nodes, configure the Chrome executable path:

  • Windows: C:\Program Files\Google\Chrome\Application\chrome.exe
  • macOS: /Applications/Google Chrome.app/Contents/MacOS/Google Chrome
  • Linux: /usr/bin/google-chrome

Comparison with Similar Tools

Section titled “Comparison with Similar Tools”
FeatureAgentic Signaln8nLangFlowFlowise
Local LLM Support✅ Native Ollama✅ With setup✅✅
No-Code Interface✅ Drag-and-drop✅✅✅
Privacy-First✅ Fully local⚠️ Hybrid⚠️ Setup needed⚠️ Setup needed
Browser Automation✅ Built-in✅❌❌
Structured Output✅ JSON schema✅✅✅
Tool Calling✅✅✅✅
LicenseDual (AGPL/Commercial)Fair CodeApache 2.0Apache 2.0

Use Cases

Section titled “Use Cases”

For Individuals

Section titled “For Individuals”
  • Personal Knowledge Management — Process and organize notes, documents, and web content
  • Task Automation — Automate repetitive tasks with AI intelligence
  • Research Assistant — Gather, analyze, and summarize information from multiple sources
  • Email Management — Categorize, prioritize, and draft responses

For Developers

Section titled “For Developers”
  • API Testing — Visual workflow builder for API integrations
  • Data Processing Pipelines — Transform and validate data with AI assistance
  • Automation Scripts — Replace complex scripts with visual workflows
  • Prototyping — Quickly test AI workflow concepts

For Businesses

Section titled “For Businesses”
  • Document Processing — Extract and analyze information from documents
  • Customer Support — Automate response drafting and categorization
  • Data Analysis — Generate insights from structured and unstructured data
  • Workflow Automation — Connect multiple services and APIs

Troubleshooting

Section titled “Troubleshooting”

App Won’t Start (Windows)

Section titled “App Won’t Start (Windows)”

If the app is slow or unresponsive on first launch:

  1. Wait for antivirus scan to complete
  2. Close and restart Agentic Signal
  3. Add exception to antivirus if needed

Ollama Connection Failed

Section titled “Ollama Connection Failed”
  1. Verify Ollama is running: ollama list
  2. Check CORS is enabled: OLLAMA_ORIGINS="*" ollama serve
  3. Verify Ollama Host in app settings is http://localhost:11434

Browser Automation Not Working

Section titled “Browser Automation Not Working”
  1. Verify Chrome is installed
  2. Check Chrome executable path in app settings
  3. Make sure Chrome version is up-to-date

Best Practices

Section titled “Best Practices”
  1. Start Simple — Begin with basic workflows before adding complexity
  2. Use Appropriate Models — Choose Ollama models based on your task (faster models for simple tasks, more capable for complex reasoning)
  3. Modular Design — Break complex workflows into smaller, reusable components
  4. Test Iteratively — Run and test each node as you build
  5. Monitor Performance — Use Data Flow Spy to debug bottlenecks

Community and Support

Section titled “Community and Support”
  • Documentation: https://agentic-signal.com/
  • GitHub: https://github.com/code-forge-temple/agentic-signal
  • Discord: Join the Agentic Signal Discord
  • Reddit: r/AgenticSignal
  • Issues: GitHub Issues
  • Discussions: GitHub Discussions

License

Section titled “License”

Dual License Model

  • Open Source (AGPL v3): Free for personal, educational, and non-commercial use
  • Commercial License: Required for business use, SaaS, or proprietary integration

See LICENSE.md for full details.

Sponsor This Project

Section titled “Sponsor This Project”

If you find Agentic Signal valuable, consider supporting development:

  • GitHub Sponsors: Sponsor code-forge-temple
  • Star the Repository: ⭐ GitHub

References

Section titled “References”
  1. Agentic Signal Official Website
  2. Agentic Signal GitHub Repository
  3. Ollama - Local LLM Runtime
  4. Quick Start Guide
  5. Nodes Reference
  6. Build AI Workflows Visually - dev.to Article
  7. Demo Video on YouTube

Published: December 29, 2025

Tags:
  • AI Workflow
  • Ollama
  • Local LLM
  • React Flow
  • Privacy-First
  • No-Code
  • Automation

Chat UI Swift: A SwiftUI Chat Application Showcasing AnyLanguageModel and Swift-HuggingFace

Dec 29, 2025
Vibe Sparking

Chat UI Swift: A SwiftUI Chat Application Showcasing AnyLanguageModel and Swift-HuggingFace

Section titled “Chat UI Swift: A SwiftUI Chat Application Showcasing AnyLanguageModel and Swift-HuggingFace”

Building AI-powered chat applications on Apple platforms has traditionally required navigating multiple SDKs, APIs, and authentication flows. mattt/chat-ui-swift simplifies this landscape with a beautiful SwiftUI reference implementation that demonstrates how to integrate multiple language model providers through a unified API.

What is Chat UI Swift?

Section titled “What is Chat UI Swift?”

Chat UI Swift is a SwiftUI chat application created by Mattt Thompson, the legendary developer behind AFNetworking and NSHipster. It serves as a demonstration app for two powerful Swift libraries:

  1. AnyLanguageModel - A drop-in replacement for Apple’s Foundation Models framework with support for custom language model providers
  2. swift-huggingface - A Swift client for the Hugging Face API

GitHub Repository: mattt/chat-ui-swift Author: Mattt Thompson License: MIT

Key Features

Section titled “Key Features”

Currently Supported

Section titled “Currently Supported”
  • Apple Intelligence - Native integration with Apple Foundation Models (macOS 26+) for on-device AI
  • Hugging Face Integration - Connect to Hugging Face with OAuth 2.0 authentication
  • Streaming Responses - Real-time streaming of AI responses for a responsive user experience
  • Chat Persistence - Save and manage multiple conversations

Planned Features

Section titled “Planned Features”
  • MLX Model Support - Download and run models locally using MLX
  • CoreML Integration - Support for CoreML-optimized models
  • GGUF Format Support - Load GGUF models from Hugging Face Hub
  • Model Downloads - Browse and download models directly from Hugging Face
  • BYOK (Bring Your Own Key) - Support for OpenAI, Anthropic, and other inference providers

Understanding AnyLanguageModel

Section titled “Understanding AnyLanguageModel”

The real power behind chat-ui-swift is AnyLanguageModel, a Swift package that provides a unified API for accessing multiple language model providers.

Why AnyLanguageModel Matters

Section titled “Why AnyLanguageModel Matters”

Traditionally, integrating different LLM providers required learning different SDKs and APIs:

With AnyLanguageModel, you use a single API:

Supported Providers

Section titled “Supported Providers”

AnyLanguageModel supports an impressive array of providers:

ProviderTypeDescription
Apple Foundation ModelsLocalOn-device AI (macOS 26+/iOS 26+)
Core MLLocalCoreML-optimized models
MLXLocalMLX models on Apple Silicon
llama.cppLocalGGUF quantized models
OllamaLocalHTTP API for local models
OpenAICloudChat Completions & Responses APIs
AnthropicCloudClaude Messages API
Google GeminiCloudGemini API

Swift 6.1 Package Traits

Section titled “Swift 6.1 Package Traits”

AnyLanguageModel leverages Swift 6.1 package traits to conditionally include heavy dependencies. This means you only compile the backends you actually use:

This results in:

  • Smaller binary sizes - Unused backends aren’t compiled
  • Faster build times - Less code to compile
  • Flexible deployment - Add/remove providers as needed

Understanding swift-huggingface

Section titled “Understanding swift-huggingface”

swift-huggingface provides comprehensive access to Hugging Face’s APIs from Swift:

Hub API

Section titled “Hub API”

Manage models, datasets, and repositories:

Inference API

Section titled “Inference API”

Run AI tasks with various models and providers:

OAuth Authentication

Section titled “OAuth Authentication”

For user-facing apps, swift-huggingface provides OAuth 2.0 authentication:

Getting Started with Chat UI Swift

Section titled “Getting Started with Chat UI Swift”

Prerequisites

Section titled “Prerequisites”
  • macOS 26 or later
  • Xcode 26+

Installation

Section titled “Installation”

Press ⌘R to build and run.

Using Hugging Face Models

Section titled “Using Hugging Face Models”
  1. Launch the app
  2. Click the sign-in button in the sidebar
  3. Authenticate with your Hugging Face account
  4. Select a Hugging Face model from the model picker
  5. Start chatting!

The app uses OAuth 2.0 to securely authenticate with Hugging Face. Your access token is stored in the Keychain and automatically refreshed.

Code Examples

Section titled “Code Examples”

Creating a Chat Session

Section titled “Creating a Chat Session”

Streaming Responses

Section titled “Streaming Responses”

Using Tools

Section titled “Using Tools”

Security Best Practices

Section titled “Security Best Practices”

The AnyLanguageModel documentation includes important guidance on API credential security:

Never Hardcode API Keys

Section titled “Never Hardcode API Keys”

Malicious actors can reverse-engineer your app binary or observe network traffic to extract embedded credentials.

Recommended Approaches

Section titled “Recommended Approaches”
  1. Bring Your Own Key (BYO) - Users provide their own keys, stored in Keychain
  2. Proxy Server - Route requests through your authenticated service

Important: Apple App Review has often rejected apps using the BYOK model. Consider using a proxy server for production apps.

Architecture Insights

Section titled “Architecture Insights”

Chat UI Swift demonstrates several important architectural patterns:

1. Protocol-Oriented Design

Section titled “1. Protocol-Oriented Design”

AnyLanguageModel uses Swift protocols to define a common interface:

2. Dependency Injection

Section titled “2. Dependency Injection”

Models are injected into sessions, making testing easy:

3. Async/Await Throughout

Section titled “3. Async/Await Throughout”

Modern Swift concurrency patterns are used throughout:

Use Cases

Section titled “Use Cases”

Chat UI Swift and its underlying libraries are ideal for:

  • AI assistant apps - Build Siri-like assistants
  • Customer service bots - Deploy support chatbots
  • Educational tools - Create tutoring applications
  • Developer tools - Build AI-powered IDE features
  • Research prototypes - Experiment with different LLMs

Comparison with Alternatives

Section titled “Comparison with Alternatives”
SolutionPlatformsProvidersOpen Source
Chat UI SwiftApple only9+ providers✅ MIT
LangChainCross-platform100+ integrations✅ MIT
Vercel AI SDKWeb-focusedMultiple providers✅ Apache 2.0
LlamaIndexCross-platformMultiple providers✅ Apache 2.0

Chat UI Swift excels in native Apple platform development with its SwiftUI-first approach and seamless integration with Apple Intelligence.

Future Directions

Section titled “Future Directions”

The project lists several planned features:

  1. MLX Support - Run MLX models locally on Apple Silicon
  2. CoreML Models - Load and run CoreML-optimized models
  3. GGUF Support - Use quantized models from Hugging Face
  4. Model Marketplace - Browse and download models in-app

Conclusion

Section titled “Conclusion”

Chat UI Swift is more than a demo app—it’s a reference implementation for building AI-powered chat applications on Apple platforms. By leveraging AnyLanguageModel and swift-huggingface, developers can:

  • Use a unified API for multiple LLM providers
  • Switch providers without changing application code
  • Integrate Apple Intelligence alongside cloud models
  • Authenticate with Hugging Face via OAuth
  • Build beautiful SwiftUI chat interfaces

The project exemplifies modern Swift development practices: protocol-oriented design, async/await concurrency, and careful attention to security.

Links

Section titled “Links”
  • GitHub: chat-ui-swift
  • GitHub: AnyLanguageModel
  • GitHub: swift-huggingface
  • Swift Package Index: AnyLanguageModel
  • InfoQ Article on AnyLanguageModel
  • Hugging Face Blog: AnyLanguageModel
Tags:
  • AI Development
  • SwiftUI
  • iOS
  • Swift
  • LLM
  • Hugging Face
  • Open Source
  • Apple Intelligence

Claude Usage Tracker: Native macOS Menu Bar App for Real-Time Claude AI Usage Monitoring

Dec 29, 2025
Vibe Sparking

Claude Usage Tracker: Native macOS Menu Bar App for Real-Time Claude AI Usage Monitoring

Section titled “Claude Usage Tracker: Native macOS Menu Bar App for Real-Time Claude AI Usage Monitoring”

For Claude AI power users, keeping track of usage limits is crucial—especially when working with the premium Opus model. Claude Usage Tracker is a beautifully crafted, native macOS menu bar application that provides real-time monitoring of your Claude AI usage limits, built entirely with Swift and SwiftUI.

Official Apple code signed • macOS 14.0+ • ~3 MB • MIT License

What is Claude Usage Tracker?

Section titled “What is Claude Usage Tracker?”

Claude Usage Tracker is a lightweight, native macOS menu bar application that provides real-time monitoring of your Claude AI usage limits. Unlike web-based solutions, this app runs natively on your Mac with:

  • Real-Time Monitoring: Track session (5-hour), weekly, and API console usage
  • Official Apple Code Signing: No security warnings—installs like any normal Mac app
  • Automatic Updates: Built-in Sparkle-powered update system
  • Developer Integration: Terminal statusline for Claude Code users
  • Privacy-First: Local storage, no telemetry, no cloud sync

GitHub Repository: hamed-elfayome/Claude-Usage-Tracker Author: Hamed Elfayome License: MIT Stars: 147+ | Install: brew install --cask claude-usage-tracker

What’s New in v2.0.0

Section titled “What’s New in v2.0.0”

The December 2024 v2.0.0 release was a major milestone that transformed the app from a great utility into a professional-grade macOS application:

FeatureDescription
Official Apple SigningNo more security workarounds—installs cleanly
Automatic UpdatesSparkle framework for seamless updates
Keychain StorageSession keys stored in macOS Keychain with auto-migration
Multi-Language Support6 languages: English, Spanish, French, German, Italian, Portuguese
Multi-Metric IconsSeparate icons for session, weekly, and API usage
Network MonitoringAuto-detects connectivity changes
Launch at LoginSystem-level auto-start option

Installation

Section titled “Installation”

Homebrew (Recommended)

Section titled “Homebrew (Recommended)”

Or tap first:

Manual Installation

Section titled “Manual Installation”
  1. Download Claude-Usage.zip
  2. Extract and drag Claude Usage.app to Applications
  3. Double-click to launch

With v2.0.0+ official Apple signing, there are no security warnings or workarounds needed!

Getting Started

Section titled “Getting Started”

1. Extract Your Session Key

Section titled “1. Extract Your Session Key”

The app needs your Claude AI session key for authentication:

  1. Open claude.ai and log in
  2. Open Developer Tools (Chrome/Edge: F12 or Cmd+Option+I)
  3. Navigate to Application → Cookies → https://claude.ai
  4. Find sessionKey cookie (starts with sk-ant-sid01-)
  5. Copy the entire value

2. First-Time Setup

Section titled “2. First-Time Setup”
  1. Launch Claude Usage Tracker
  2. The Welcome Wizard appears automatically
  3. Paste your session key and click “Validate”
  4. Wait for confirmation (green checkmark)
  5. Click “Done”

The menu bar icon now shows your live usage statistics!

Key Features

Section titled “Key Features”

Customizable Display

Section titled “Customizable Display”

Choose from 5 icon styles:

  • Battery Style: Classic indicator with fill level
  • Progress Bar: Horizontal bar with percentage
  • Percentage Only: Minimalist text display
  • Icon with Bar: Claude icon + progress indicator
  • Compact: Space-efficient for crowded menu bars

Plus Multi-Metric Icons: Display separate icons for session, weekly, and API usage simultaneously.

Smart Automation

Section titled “Smart Automation”
  • Auto-Start Sessions: Automatically initialize new sessions when usage resets
  • Threshold Notifications: Alerts at 75%, 90%, and 95% usage
  • Configurable Refresh: Set intervals from 5 to 120 seconds
  • Launch at Login: System-level auto-start

Claude Code Integration

Section titled “Claude Code Integration”

For developers using Claude Code, the app provides terminal statusline integration:

Display components:

  • Current directory name
  • Git branch with ⎇ icon
  • Usage percentage with color coding
  • 10-segment progress bar
  • Reset timer countdown

Setup is one-click from Settings → Claude Code tab.

Security & Privacy

Section titled “Security & Privacy”
  • macOS Keychain: Session keys stored securely
  • Automatic Migration: v2.0+ migrates old storage to Keychain
  • Official Apple Signing: Verified authenticity
  • Local Only: No cloud sync, zero telemetry
  • HTTPS-Only: Secure API communication

Usage Scenarios

Section titled “Usage Scenarios”

For Writers & Content Creators

Section titled “For Writers & Content Creators”

Track your 5-hour session usage to avoid interruptions during deep work sessions. The color-coded indicators (green → orange → red) give instant visual feedback.

For Developers

Section titled “For Developers”

Monitor both web and API console usage when integrating Claude into applications. The terminal statusline keeps usage visible without leaving your workflow.

For Teams

Section titled “For Teams”

Share consistent usage tracking across team members with the standardized Homebrew installation. The multi-language support ensures accessibility for international teams.

Technical Details

Section titled “Technical Details”

Technology Stack

Section titled “Technology Stack”
ComponentTechnology
LanguageSwift 5.0+
UI FrameworkSwiftUI 5.0+
PlatformmacOS 14.0+ (Sonoma)
ArchitectureMVVM with Protocol-Oriented Design
StorageUserDefaults with App Groups
NetworkingURLSession with async/await
UpdatesSparkle Framework

API Integration

Section titled “API Integration”

The app integrates with multiple Claude API endpoints:

Response includes:

  • five_hour: 5-hour rolling session usage
  • seven_day: Weekly usage across all models
  • seven_day_opus: Opus-specific weekly usage
  • extra_usage: Claude Extra cost tracking

Building from Source

Section titled “Building from Source”

Contributing

Section titled “Contributing”

Contributions are welcome! The project follows:

  • Swift API Design Guidelines
  • SwiftUI best practices
  • MVVM architecture
  • Comprehensive test coverage

Why Claude Usage Tracker Stands Out

Section titled “Why Claude Usage Tracker Stands Out”
FeatureClaude Usage TrackerWeb Dashboards
Native PerformanceLightweight Swift/SwiftUIBrowser overhead
Always VisibleMenu bar iconRequires browser tab
Privacy100% local, no telemetryCloud-dependent
AutomationAuto-start, notificationsManual checking
Developer IntegrationClaude Code statuslineNo terminal support
InstallationOne-line HomebrewBrowser extensions

Conclusion

Section titled “Conclusion”

Claude Usage Tracker fills a crucial gap for Claude AI power users on macOS. The v2.0.0 release elevated it from a useful utility to a professional-grade application with official Apple signing, automatic updates, and robust security.

Whether you’re a writer managing session limits, a developer monitoring API usage, or part of a team standardizing on Claude AI, this native macOS app provides the elegant, privacy-first solution you need.

Install today:

GitHub: hamed-elfayome/Claude-Usage-Tracker

Disclaimer: This application is not affiliated with, endorsed by, or sponsored by Anthropic PBC. Claude is a trademark of Anthropic PBC.

Tags:
  • AI Development
  • macOS App
  • Swift
  • SwiftUI
  • Claude AI
  • Open Source

ClaudeCode Workflow Studio: Visual Automation Editor for AI Workflows

Dec 29, 2025
Vibe Sparking

ClaudeCode Workflow Studio: Visual Automation Editor for AI Workflows

Section titled “ClaudeCode Workflow Studio: Visual Automation Editor for AI Workflows”

Claude Code, Anthropic’s official CLI tool for AI-assisted development, has a powerful automation system through its .claude configuration files. However, creating complex workflows with multiple sub-agents, conditional branching, and user interactions requires editing markdown and YAML files manually. ClaudeCode Workflow Studio (cc-wf-studio) by breaking-brake brings a visual, drag-and-drop editor to VSCode, making workflow creation intuitive and accessible.

What is ClaudeCode Workflow Studio?

Section titled “What is ClaudeCode Workflow Studio?”

ClaudeCode Workflow Studio is a VSCode extension that provides a visual workflow editor for Claude Code. It allows you to:

  • Design workflows visually - Drag and drop nodes onto a canvas
  • Configure without code - Set up sub-agents, skills, and MCP tools through a UI
  • Export with one click - Generate .claude/agents/ and .claude/commands/ files automatically
  • Collaborate with your team - Share workflows via Slack with preview cards

GitHub Repository: breaking-brake/cc-wf-studio VSCode Marketplace: Open VSX License: AGPL-3.0-or-later Version: 3.9.1 (as of December 2025)

Key Features

Section titled “Key Features”

1. Visual Workflow Editor

Section titled “1. Visual Workflow Editor”

The core of cc-wf-studio is its drag-and-drop canvas. Instead of writing markdown and YAML, you visually connect nodes to define workflow logic:

  • Prompt Nodes - Define reusable prompt templates with {{variable}} substitution
  • Sub-Agent Nodes - Configure autonomous AI agents with custom system prompts and tool permissions
  • Skill Nodes - Integrate Claude Code Skills from personal or project directories
  • MCP Nodes - Connect to Model Context Protocol tools for external integrations
  • Control Flow Nodes - IfElse (binary branching), Switch (multi-way branching), AskUserQuestion (user decisions)

2. AI-Assisted Workflow Refinement

Section titled “2. AI-Assisted Workflow Refinement”

One of the standout features is AI-assisted refinement. Instead of generating workflows from scratch once, you can iteratively improve them through conversation:

  1. Click the “Edit with AI” button (✨ sparkle icon)
  2. Describe your changes in natural language (max 2000 characters)
  3. The AI modifies your workflow incrementally, preserving existing work
  4. Repeat until satisfied

This conversational approach builds workflows through multiple rounds of feedback, with the AI maintaining conversation history and applying changes incrementally.

Example prompts:

  • “Add a Sub-Agent node that validates the input data”
  • “Connect the error handler to a logging Sub-Agent”
  • “Change the AskUserQuestion node to have 3 options: High, Medium, Low”

3. One-Click Export

Section titled “3. One-Click Export”

When your workflow is ready, click Export to generate:

  • .claude/agents/*.md - Sub-Agent definitions with system prompts and tool configurations
  • .claude/commands/*.md - SlashCommand definitions that execute the entire workflow

The exported files are ready to use immediately with Claude Code CLI. No manual editing required.

4. Slack Workflow Sharing (Beta)

Section titled “4. Slack Workflow Sharing (Beta)”

For teams, cc-wf-studio offers Slack integration:

  • Export workflows to Slack channels with preview cards
  • One-click import links for team members
  • Seamless collaboration without leaving your chat workspace

Installation

Section titled “Installation”

From VSCode Marketplace (Recommended)

Section titled “From VSCode Marketplace (Recommended)”
  1. Open VSCode Extensions (Ctrl+Shift+X / Cmd+Shift+X)
  2. Search for “Claude Code Workflow Studio”
  3. Click Install

From Open VSX

Section titled “From Open VSX”

The extension is also available on Open VSX:

  1. Install the Open VSX extension
  2. Search for “cc-wf-studio”
  3. Click Install

From Source

Section titled “From Source”

  1. Clone the repository:

  2. Install dependencies:

  3. Build the extension:

  4. Package and install:

  5. Install the generated .vsix file through VSCode’s “Install from VSIX…” option

Quick Start Guide

Section titled “Quick Start Guide”

1. Open the Editor

Section titled “1. Open the Editor”
  • Press Ctrl+Shift+P / Cmd+Shift+P
